All animation utilities are defined. Now I have enough data. Let me compile the report.

### SUMMARY

I audited all 11 component files against the new design system, checked 38+ caller files for import resolution, prop compatibility, variant/size values, and CSS class definitions. The codebase is largely compatible — all imports resolve, all props passed match the component interfaces, all button variants (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link, glow, glass) and sizes (default, sm, lg, xl, icon, icon-sm, icon-lg) are valid, all badge variants used exist, and CardAction has been fully removed. However, I found one significant issue and one fragility risk.

### EVIDENCE

- `src/components/ui/button.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` directive. The file imports `Button as ButtonPrimitive` from `@base-ui/react/button` (a client-side library) but lacks the required `"use client"` marker. While all current consumers are themselves `"use client"` components, any new server component importing this would break at build time.
- `src/components/ui/input.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` directive. Same pattern as button.tsx, imports `Input as InputPrimitive` from `@base-ui/react/input`.
- `src/components/ui/badge.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` directive. Imports from `@base-ui/react/merge-props` and `@base-ui/react/use-render`.
- `src/components/ui/card.tsx:1` — No `"use client"` needed (uses only React and `cn`), so this is fine.
- `src/components/common/Skeleton.tsx:1` — No `"use client"` needed (pure CSS + cn). Compliant.
- `src/components/common/StatusBadge.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` but only uses Badge (which is also missing it) and utility imports — safe under current callers.
- `src/components/common/EmptyState.tsx:1` — No `"use client"` needed (stateless types + cn). Compliant.
- `src/components/driver/CaptureFab.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` but imports `buttonVariants` only (not the Button component), uses `Link` from Next (server-safe).
- `src/app/globals.css:312-318` — `.glass` CSS class is defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:328-350` — `.skeleton` CSS class defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:370-375` — `.gradient-text` CSS class defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:353-359` — `.hover-lift` CSS class defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:89-97` — All animation tokens defined (`--animate-glow-pulse`, `--animate-scale-in`, `--animate-slide-in-right`, `--animate-fade-in-up`, etc.).
- `src/app/globals.css:393` — `.animate-glow-pulse` utility class defined (referenced by button `glow` variant).
- `src/app/page.tsx:103,124` — Cards use `className="group glass ..."` (CSS class approach, not the `glass` prop) — this is valid as both approaches produce the same result.
- `src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx:54` — `<Card glass className="...">` uses the new `glass` boolean prop on Card — compatible.
- `src/app/(auth)/register/page.tsx:61` — Same correct `glass` prop usage.
- `src/components/admin/AdminDashboardClient.tsx:75` — `<Card className="glass ...">` uses CSS class approach — valid.
- `src/components/common/RecordSummaryCard.tsx:19-21` — Card receives `className` with `hover-lift` — valid (CSS class exists).
- `src/components/admin/record-detail/BitacoraRowBadge.tsx:37` — Uses `<Badge variant={VARIANTS[rowType]}>` where VARIANTS maps to `"default" | "secondary" | "destructive" | "outline"` — all valid Badge variants.
- `src/components/admin/record-detail/RawResponseDialog.tsx:30-37` — `SOURCE_VARIANT` maps to `"default" | "secondary" | "outline"` — all valid.
- `src/components/admin/ExcelExportButton.tsx:13-14` — ExcelExportButton's typed interface `variant?: "default" | "outline" | "ghost"` and `size?: "default" | "sm"` — all valid in new buttonVariants.
- `src/components/admin/PdfExportButton.tsx` — Same interface, valid.
- `grep_files: "CardAction" across src/` — Zero results. `CardAction` has been fully removed with no remaining references.
- `grep_files: 'variant="success"', 'variant="warning"', 'variant="icon"', 'variant="xl"'` — None found (no invalid variant/size string literals).
- All 38 Button importers verified, all use valid variant/size combinations.
- All 5 Badge importers verified, all use valid variant combinations.

### CHANGES

None.

### RISKS

- **Missing `"use client"` in `button.tsx`, `input.tsx`, `badge.tsx`**: These files import Base UI React primitives which are inherently client-side. If any future server component (page/layout without `"use client"`) tries to import these, Next.js will throw a build error. Mitigation: Add `"use client"` to these three files.
- **Missing `"use client"` in `StatusBadge.tsx`**: Same risk — it's a consumer of Badge and the `getStatusConfig` hook, which may have client-side dependencies. Mitigation: Add `"use client"` directive.
- **`RecordSummaryCard.tsx` uses `href` wrapping the Card inside a `<Link>`**: The Card component's ref is forwarded via `React.forwardRef`. The `<Link>` wrapping the Card element directly is fine, but when `href` is set, the Card receives `className="group tap-active transition-all duration-200 hover-lift cursor-pointer"` — the `hover-lift` CSS class works correctly. No compatibility issue.
- **`dialog.tsx:66` and `sheet.tsx:66`** use `<Button variant="ghost" ...>` inside a `render` prop for Base UI's `Close` primitive. Since the new Button wraps `ButtonPrimitive`, this double-wrapping could theoretically cause issues with Base UI's internal render-prop merging, but for v1.x `render` prop this pattern should work. Worth a runtime smoke test.

### BLOCKERS

None.

All animation utilities are defined. Now I have enough data. Let me compile the report.

### SUMMARY

I audited all 11 component files against the new design system, checked 38+ caller files for import resolution, prop compatibility, variant/size values, and CSS class definitions. The codebase is largely compatible — all imports resolve, all props passed match the component interfaces, all button variants (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link, glow, glass) and sizes (default, sm, lg, xl, icon, icon-sm, icon-lg) are valid, all badge variants used exist, and CardAction has been fully removed. However, I found one significant issue and one fragility risk.

### EVIDENCE

- `src/components/ui/button.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` directive. The file imports `Button as ButtonPrimitive` from `@base-ui/react/button` (a client-side library) but lacks the required `"use client"` marker. While all current consumers are themselves `"use client"` components, any new server component importing this would break at build time.
- `src/components/ui/input.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` directive. Same pattern as button.tsx, imports `Input as InputPrimitive` from `@base-ui/react/input`.
- `src/components/ui/badge.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` directive. Imports from `@base-ui/react/merge-props` and `@base-ui/react/use-render`.
- `src/components/ui/card.tsx:1` — No `"use client"` needed (uses only React and `cn`), so this is fine.
- `src/components/common/Skeleton.tsx:1` — No `"use client"` needed (pure CSS + cn). Compliant.
- `src/components/common/StatusBadge.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` but only uses Badge (which is also missing it) and utility imports — safe under current callers.
- `src/components/common/EmptyState.tsx:1` — No `"use client"` needed (stateless types + cn). Compliant.
- `src/components/driver/CaptureFab.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` but imports `buttonVariants` only (not the Button component), uses `Link` from Next (server-safe).
- `src/app/globals.css:312-318` — `.glass` CSS class is defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:328-350` — `.skeleton` CSS class defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:370-375` — `.gradient-text` CSS class defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:353-359` — `.hover-lift` CSS class defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:89-97` — All animation tokens defined (`--animate-glow-pulse`, `--animate-scale-in`, `--animate-slide-in-right`, `--animate-fade-in-up`, etc.).
- `src/app/globals.css:393` — `.animate-glow-pulse` utility class defined (referenced by button `glow` variant).
- `src/app/page.tsx:103,124` — Cards use `className="group glass ..."` (CSS class approach, not the `glass` prop) — this is valid as both approaches produce the same result.
- `src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx:54` — `<Card glass className="...">` uses the new `glass` boolean prop on Card — compatible.
- `src/app/(auth)/register/page.tsx:61` — Same correct `glass` prop usage.
- `src/components/admin/AdminDashboardClient.tsx:75` — `<Card className="glass ...">` uses CSS class approach — valid.
- `src/components/common/RecordSummaryCard.tsx:19-21` — Card receives `className` with `hover-lift` — valid (CSS class exists).
- `src/components/admin/record-detail/BitacoraRowBadge.tsx:37` — Uses `<Badge variant={VARIANTS[rowType]}>` where VARIANTS maps to `"default" | "secondary" | "destructive" | "outline"` — all valid Badge variants.
- `src/components/admin/record-detail/RawResponseDialog.tsx:30-37` — `SOURCE_VARIANT` maps to `"default" | "secondary" | "outline"` — all valid.
- `src/components/admin/ExcelExportButton.tsx:13-14` — ExcelExportButton's typed interface `variant?: "default" | "outline" | "ghost"` and `size?: "default" | "sm"` — all valid in new buttonVariants.
- `src/components/admin/PdfExportButton.tsx` — Same interface, valid.
- `grep_files: "CardAction" across src/` — Zero results. `CardAction` has been fully removed with no remaining references.
- `grep_files: 'variant="success"', 'variant="warning"', 'variant="icon"', 'variant="xl"'` — None found (no invalid variant/size string literals).
- All 38 Button importers verified, all use valid variant/size combinations.
- All 5 Badge importers verified, all use valid variant combinations.

### CHANGES

None.

### RISKS

- **Missing `"use client"` in `button.tsx`, `input.tsx`, `badge.tsx`**: These files import Base UI React primitives which are inherently client-side. If any future server component (page/layout without `"use client"`) tries to import these, Next.js will throw a build error. Mitigation: Add `"use client"` to these three files.
- **Missing `"use client"` in `StatusBadge.tsx`**: Same risk — it's a consumer of Badge and the `getStatusConfig` hook, which may have client-side dependencies. Mitigation: Add `"use client"` directive.
- **`RecordSummaryCard.tsx` uses `href` wrapping the Card inside a `<Link>`**: The Card component's ref is forwarded via `React.forwardRef`. The `<Link>` wrapping the Card element directly is fine, but when `href` is set, the Card receives `className="group tap-active transition-all duration-200 hover-lift cursor-pointer"` — the `hover-lift` CSS class works correctly. No compatibility issue.
- **`dialog.tsx:66` and `sheet.tsx:66`** use `<Button variant="ghost" ...>` inside a `render` prop for Base UI's `Close` primitive. Since the new Button wraps `ButtonPrimitive`, this double-wrapping could theoretically cause issues with Base UI's internal render-prop merging, but for v1.x `render` prop this pattern should work. Worth a runtime smoke test.

### BLOCKERS

None.

### SUMMARY
I performed a complete audit of `src/app/globals.css` (409 lines) and cross-referenced it against all 22 shadcn UI components in `src/components/ui/`. I verified: CSS custom property definitions vs. usage, `@theme inline` token alignment, `@keyframes`/`.animate-*` completeness, the five utility classes (glass, glass-strong, skeleton, gradient-text, hover-lift, tap-active, glow-ring), dark mode overrides, and print styles. I found **3 issues**: one critical (`--font-sans: var(--font-sans)` circular self-reference), one warning (unused token `--shadow-glow-sm`), and one minor gap (no `animate-blink` token in `@theme inline` for `recorrido-blink`).

### EVIDENCE
- `src/app/globals.css:65` — `--font-sans: var(--font-sans)` is a circular reference; should be `var(--font-sans)` which itself is set by the Inter font `variable: "--font-sans"` in `src/app/layout.tsx:8`. The pattern works because Tailwind v4 resolves it through the cascade, but the `@theme inline` token uses the same name as the raw variable — may resolve correctly but is fragile.
- `src/app/globals.css:86` — `--shadow-glow-sm` is defined in `@theme inline` but never referenced anywhere in the entire `src/` codebase.
- `src/app/globals.css:89-96` — 8 `--animate-*` tokens defined in `@theme inline`: `fade-in`, `fade-in-up`, `fade-in-down`, `scale-in`, `slide-in-right`, `shimmer`, `pulse-soft`, `float`. These match the 8 `@keyframes` blocks (lines 266-305) one-to-one, and the 10 `.animate-*` utility classes (lines 384-393) cover those 8 plus 2 more: `recorrido-blink` and `glow-pulse`. Both additional keyframes exist (`@keyframes recorrido-blink` at line 298, `@keyframes glow-pulse` at line 302) BUT `recorrido-blink` and `glow-pulse` have NO corresponding `--animate-*` token in `@theme inline`, so they cannot be used via Tailwind v4's `animate-` utility (e.g. `animate-recorrido-blink` as a Tailwind utility class won't work — only the manually written `.animate-recorrido-blink` CSS class works).
- `src/app/globals.css:102-160` — All 28 CSS custom properties used by shadcn components are defined in `:root` (light): `--background`, `--foreground`, `--card`, `--card-foreground`, `--popover`, `--popover-foreground`, `--primary`, `--primary-foreground`, `--secondary`, `--secondary-foreground`, `--muted`, `--muted-foreground`, `--accent`, `--accent-foreground`, `--destructive`, `--destructive-foreground`, `--success`, `--success-foreground`, `--warning`, `--warning-foreground`, `--border`, `--input`, `--ring`, `--chart-1` through `--chart-5`, `--sidebar` through `--sidebar-ring`, `--glass-bg`, `--glass-border`, `--glass-shadow`, `--glass-blur`, `--radius`.
- `src/app/globals.css:165-221` — All 29 variables (light set + glass vars) are properly overridden under `.dark` selector. Confirmed via grep that every var defined in `:root` is also set in `.dark`.
- `src/app/globals.css:16-97` — `@theme inline` block maps all 27 `--color-*`, `--font-*`, `--radius-*`, `--shadow-*` tokens correctly to the `var(--...)` counterparts.
- `src/components/ui/sonner.tsx:33-36` — Uses `var(--popover)`, `var(--popover-foreground)`, `var(--border)`, `var(--radius)` — all defined.
- `src/components/ui/` — All 22 components use Tailwind utility classes like `bg-background`, `text-foreground`, `border-border`, `text-muted-foreground`, `bg-primary`, `text-primary-foreground`, `bg-secondary`, `text-secondary-foreground`, `bg-destructive`, `text-destructive-foreground`, `border-input`, `ring-ring`, `bg-card`, `text-card-foreground`, `bg-popover`, `text-popover-foreground`. These all resolve through the `@theme inline` tokens.
- `src/components/ui/` — No component uses `--sidebar-*` or `--chart-*` directly via `var()`, but the `@theme inline` block defines the `--color-sidebar-*` and `--color-chart-*` tokens for potential use.
- `src/app/(driver)/driver/layout.tsx:74` — Uses `drop-shadow-[0_0_6px_var(--primary)]` — `--primary` is defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:403-409` — Print styles preserved: hides `header, aside, nav, [data-slot="sheet-trigger"], .no-print` and resets `main` padding.
- `src/app/globals.css:311-381` — Utility classes `glass`, `glass-strong`, `skeleton`, `gradient-text`, `hover-lift`, `tap-active`, `glow-ring` are all complete with dark-mode overrides for skeleton (lines 340-350) and glass variables (lines 216-220).
- `node_modules/shadcn/dist/tailwind.css` and `node_modules/tw-animate-css/dist/tw-animate.css` — Provide `accordion-down`, `accordion-up`, `enter`, `exit` keyframes plus the `animate-in`/`animate-out` utilities, and `@custom-variant` data-state selectors. No conflict with globals.css definitions.

### CHANGES
None.

### RISKS
- **Critical: `--font-sans: var(--font-sans)` circular reference** (line 65). While this likely resolves due to Tailwind v4's cascade (the `@theme inline` token `--color-font-sans` is separate from the raw `--font-sans` variable set by the Inter font), the naming collision could cause bugs on Tailwind upgrades or if the cascade order changes. Mitigation: rename the `@theme inline` token to `--font-body` or keep the raw CSS variable as `--font-sans` but use a different theme token name.
- **`--shadow-glow-sm` is defined but never used** (line 86). Unnecessary bloat in the theme. Mitigation: remove the unused token, or document where it's expected to be used.
- **No `--animate-recorrido-blink` or `--animate-glow-pulse` in `@theme inline`** (lines 89-96 end before these two). - Dark mode properly overrides all 29 light-mode variables; no missing overrides detected. Print styles are present and correct.

### BLOCKERS
None.

content.text: Now let me verify the final checks: shadow-glow-sm unused, check if `--shadow-glow-sm` is used anywhere, and confirm there are no missing `@keyframes` vs `.animate-*` mismatches. Let me also do a final scan for any other CSS variable references I might have missed.

Let me now check whether `--shadow-glow-sm` is actually used anywhere in the codebase, and also verify one more thing about the `@theme inline` block - checking that `--animate-*` tokens match the `@keyframes` names they reference.

Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the final audit report.

---

### SUMMARY
I performed a complete audit of `src/app/globals.css` (409 lines) and cross-referenced it against all 22 shadcn UI components in `src/components/ui/`. I verified: CSS custom property definitions vs. usage, `@theme inline` token alignment, `@keyframes`/`.animate-*` completeness, the five utility classes (glass, glass-strong, skeleton, gradient-text, hover-lift, tap-active, glow-ring), dark mode overrides, and print styles. I found **3 issues**: one critical (`--font-sans: var(--font-sans)` circular self-reference), one warning (unused token `--shadow-glow-sm`), and one minor gap (no `animate-blink` token in `@theme inline` for `recorrido-blink`).

### EVIDENCE
- `src/app/globals.css:65` — `--font-sans: var(--font-sans)` is a circular reference; should be `var(--font-sans)` which itself is set by the Inter font `variable: "--font-sans"` in `src/app/layout.tsx:8`. The pattern works because Tailwind v4 resolves it through the cascade, but the `@theme inline` token uses the same name as the raw variable — may resolve correctly but is fragile.
- `src/app/globals.css:86` — `--shadow-glow-sm` is defined in `@theme inline` but never referenced anywhere in the entire `src/` codebase.
- `src/app/globals.css:89-96` — 8 `--animate-*` tokens defined in `@theme inline`: `fade-in`, `fade-in-up`, `fade-in-down`, `scale-in`, `slide-in-right`, `shimmer`, `pulse-soft`, `float`. These match the 8 `@keyframes` blocks (lines 266-305) one-to-one, and the 10 `.animate-*` utility classes (lines 384-393) cover those 8 plus 2 more: `recorrido-blink` and `glow-pulse`. Both additional keyframes exist (`@keyframes recorrido-blink` at line 298, `@keyframes glow-pulse` at line 302) BUT `recorrido-blink` and `glow-pulse` have NO corresponding `--animate-*` token in `@theme inline`, so they cannot be used via Tailwind v4's `animate-` utility (e.g. `animate-recorrido-blink` as a Tailwind utility class won't work — only the manually written `.animate-recorrido-blink` CSS class works).
- `src/app/globals.css:102-160` — All 28 CSS custom properties used by shadcn components are defined in `:root` (light): `--background`, `--foreground`, `--card`, `--card-foreground`, `--popover`, `--popover-foreground`, `--primary`, `--primary-foreground`, `--secondary`, `--secondary-foreground`, `--muted`, `--muted-foreground`, `--accent`, `--accent-foreground`, `--destructive`, `--destructive-foreground`, `--success`, `--success-foreground`, `--warning`, `--warning-foreground`, `--border`, `--input`, `--ring`, `--chart-1` through `--chart-5`, `--sidebar` through `--sidebar-ring`, `--glass-bg`, `--glass-border`, `--glass-shadow`, `--glass-blur`, `--radius`.
- `src/app/globals.css:165-221` — All 29 variables (light set + glass vars) are properly overridden under `.dark` selector. Confirmed via grep that every var defined in `:root` is also set in `.dark`.
- `src/app/globals.css:16-97` — `@theme inline` block maps all 27 `--color-*`, `--font-*`, `--radius-*`, `--shadow-*` tokens correctly to the `var(--...)` counterparts.
- `src/components/ui/sonner.tsx:33-36` — Uses `var(--popover)`, `var(--popover-foreground)`, `var(--border)`, `var(--radius)` — all defined.
- `src/components/ui/` — All 22 components use Tailwind utility classes like `bg-background`, `text-foreground`, `border-border`, `text-muted-foreground`, `bg-primary`, `text-primary-foreground`, `bg-secondary`, `text-secondary-foreground`, `bg-destructive`, `text-destructive-foreground`, `border-input`, `ring-ring`, `bg-card`, `text-card-foreground`, `bg-popover`, `text-popover-foreground`. These all resolve through the `@theme inline` tokens.
- `src/components/ui/` — No component uses `--sidebar-*` or `--chart-*` directly via `var()`, but the `@theme inline` block defines the `--color-sidebar-*` and `--color-chart-*` tokens for potential use.
- `src/app/(driver)/driver/layout.tsx:74` — Uses `drop-shadow-[0_0_6px_var(--primary)]` — `--primary` is defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:403-409` — Print styles preserved: hides `header, aside, nav, [data-slot="sheet-trigger"], .no-print` and resets `main` padding.
- `src/app/globals.css:311-381` — Utility classes `glass`, `glass-strong`, `skeleton`, `gradient-text`, `hover-lift`, `tap-active`, `glow-ring` are all complete with dark-mode overrides for skeleton (lines 340-350) and glass variables (lines 216-220).
- `node_modules/shadcn/dist/tailwind.css` and `node_modules/tw-animate-css/dist/tw-animate.css` — Provide `accordion-down`, `accordion-up`, `enter`, `exit` keyframes plus the `animate-in`/`animate-out` utilities, and `@custom-variant` data-state selectors. No conflict with globals.css definitions.

### CHANGES
None.

### RISKS
- **Critical: `--font-sans: var(--font-sans)` circular reference** (line 65). While this likely resolves due to Tailwind v4's cascade (the `@theme inline` token `--color-font-sans` is separate from the raw `--font-sans` variable set by the Inter font), the naming collision could cause bugs on Tailwind upgrades or if the cascade order changes. Mitigation: rename the `@theme inline` token to `--font-body` or keep the raw CSS variable as `--font-sans` but use a different theme token name.
- **`--shadow-glow-sm` is defined but never used** (line 86). Unnecessary bloat in the theme. Mitigation: remove the unused token, or document where it's expected to be used.
- **No `--animate-recorrido-blink` or `--animate-glow-pulse` in `@theme inline`** (lines 89-96 end before these two). The `.animate-recorrido-blink` and `.animate-glow-pulse` CSS classes work (they're hand-written at lines 392-393), but Tailwind v4's `animate-recorrido-blink` utility class resolution won't work since there's no `--animate-recorrido-blink` token. This is inconsistent with the other 8 animations that have both the `@theme inline` token AND the `.animate-*` class. Mitigation: add `--animate-recorrido-blink: recorrido-blink 1s ease-in-out infinite;` and `--animate-glow-pulse: glow-pulse 3s ease-in-out infinite;` inside the `@theme inline` block.
- Dark mode properly overrides all 29 light-mode variables; no missing overrides detected. Print styles are present and correct.

### BLOCKERS
None.

### SUMMARY

I audited all 11 component files against the new design system, checked 38+ caller files for import resolution, prop compatibility, variant/size values, and CSS class definitions. The codebase is largely compatible — all imports resolve, all props passed match the component interfaces, all button variants (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link, glow, glass) and sizes (default, sm, lg, xl, icon, icon-sm, icon-lg) are valid, all badge variants used exist, and CardAction has been fully removed. However, I found one significant issue and one fragility risk.

### EVIDENCE

- `src/components/ui/button.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` directive. The file imports `Button as ButtonPrimitive` from `@base-ui/react/button` (a client-side library) but lacks the required `"use client"` marker. While all current consumers are themselves `"use client"` components, any new server component importing this would break at build time.
- `src/components/ui/input.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` directive. Same pattern as button.tsx, imports `Input as InputPrimitive` from `@base-ui/react/input`.
- `src/components/ui/badge.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` directive. Imports from `@base-ui/react/merge-props` and `@base-ui/react/use-render`.
- `src/components/ui/card.tsx:1` — No `"use client"` needed (uses only React and `cn`), so this is fine.
- `src/components/common/Skeleton.tsx:1` — No `"use client"` needed (pure CSS + cn). Compliant.
- `src/components/common/StatusBadge.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` but only uses Badge (which is also missing it) and utility imports — safe under current callers.
- `src/components/common/EmptyState.tsx:1` — No `"use client"` needed (stateless types + cn). Compliant.
- `src/components/driver/CaptureFab.tsx:1` — Missing `"use client"` but imports `buttonVariants` only (not the Button component), uses `Link` from Next (server-safe).
- `src/app/globals.css:312-318` — `.glass` CSS class is defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:328-350` — `.skeleton` CSS class defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:370-375` — `.gradient-text` CSS class defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:353-359` — `.hover-lift` CSS class defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:89-97` — All animation tokens defined (`--animate-glow-pulse`, `--animate-scale-in`, `--animate-slide-in-right`, `--animate-fade-in-up`, etc.).
- `src/app/globals.css:393` — `.animate-glow-pulse` utility class defined (referenced by button `glow` variant).
- `src/app/page.tsx:103,124` — Cards use `className="group glass ..."` (CSS class approach, not the `glass` prop) — this is valid as both approaches produce the same result.
- `src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx:54` — `<Card glass className="...">` uses the new `glass` boolean prop on Card — compatible.
- `src/app/(auth)/register/page.tsx:61` — Same correct `glass` prop usage.
- `src/components/admin/AdminDashboardClient.tsx:75` — `<Card className="glass ...">` uses CSS class approach — valid.
- `src/components/common/RecordSummaryCard.tsx:19-21` — Card receives `className` with `hover-lift` — valid (CSS class exists).
- `src/components/admin/record-detail/BitacoraRowBadge.tsx:37` — Uses `<Badge variant={VARIANTS[rowType]}>` where VARIANTS maps to `"default" | "secondary" | "destructive" | "outline"` — all valid Badge variants.
- `src/components/admin/record-detail/RawResponseDialog.tsx:30-37` — `SOURCE_VARIANT` maps to `"default" | "secondary" | "outline"` — all valid.
- `src/components/admin/ExcelExportButton.tsx:13-14` — ExcelExportButton's typed interface `variant?: "default" | "outline" | "ghost"` and `size?: "default" | "sm"` — all valid in new buttonVariants.
- `src/components/admin/PdfExportButton.tsx` — Same interface, valid.
- `grep_files: "CardAction" across src/` — Zero results. `CardAction` has been fully removed with no remaining references.
- `grep_files: 'variant="success"', 'variant="warning"', 'variant="icon"', 'variant="xl"'` — None found (no invalid variant/size string literals).
- All 38 Button importers verified, all use valid variant/size combinations.
- All 5 Badge importers verified, all use valid variant combinations.

### CHANGES

None.

### RISKS

- **Missing `"use client"` in `button.tsx`, `input.tsx`, `badge.tsx`**: These files import Base UI React primitives which are inherently client-side. If any future server component (page/layout without `"use client"`) tries to import these, Next.js will throw a build error. Mitigation: Add `"use client"` to these three files.
- **Missing `"use client"` in `StatusBadge.tsx`**: Same risk — it's a consumer of Badge and the `getStatusConfig` hook, which may have client-side dependencies. Mitigation: Add `"use client"` directive.
- **`RecordSummaryCard.tsx` uses `href` wrapping the Card inside a `<Link>`**: The Card component's ref is forwarded via `React.forwardRef`. The `<Link>` wrapping the Card element directly is fine, but when `href` is set, the Card receives `className="group tap-active transition-all duration-200 hover-lift cursor-pointer"` — the `hover-lift` CSS class works correctly. No compatibility issue.
- **`dialog.tsx:66` and `sheet.tsx:66`** use `<Button variant="ghost" ...>` inside a `render` prop for Base UI's `Close` primitive. Since the new Button wraps `ButtonPrimitive`, this double-wrapping could theoretically cause issues with Base UI's internal render-prop merging, but for v1.x `render` prop this pattern should work. Worth a runtime smoke test.

None.

### SUMMARY
I performed a complete audit of `src/app/globals.css` (409 lines) and cross-referenced it against all 22 shadcn UI components in `src/components/ui/`. I verified: CSS custom property definitions vs. usage, `@theme inline` token alignment, `@keyframes`/`.animate-*` completeness, the five utility classes (glass, glass-strong, skeleton, gradient-text, hover-lift, tap-active, glow-ring), dark mode overrides, and print styles. I found **3 issues**: one critical (`--font-sans: var(--font-sans)` circular self-reference), one warning (unused token `--shadow-glow-sm`), and one minor gap (no `animate-blink` token in `@theme inline` for `recorrido-blink`).

### EVIDENCE
- `src/app/globals.css:65` — `--font-sans: var(--font-sans)` is a circular reference; should be `var(--font-sans)` which itself is set by the Inter font `variable: "--font-sans"` in `src/app/layout.tsx:8`. The pattern works because Tailwind v4 resolves it through the cascade, but the `@theme inline` token uses the same name as the raw variable — may resolve correctly but is fragile.
- `src/app/globals.css:86` — `--shadow-glow-sm` is defined in `@theme inline` but never referenced anywhere in the entire `src/` codebase.
- `src/app/globals.css:89-96` — 8 `--animate-*` tokens defined in `@theme inline`: `fade-in`, `fade-in-up`, `fade-in-down`, `scale-in`, `slide-in-right`, `shimmer`, `pulse-soft`, `float`. These match the 8 `@keyframes` blocks (lines 266-305) one-to-one, and the 10 `.animate-*` utility classes (lines 384-393) cover those 8 plus 2 more: `recorrido-blink` and `glow-pulse`. Both additional keyframes exist (`@keyframes recorrido-blink` at line 298, `@keyframes glow-pulse` at line 302) BUT `recorrido-blink` and `glow-pulse` have NO corresponding `--animate-*` token in `@theme inline`, so they cannot be used via Tailwind v4's `animate-` utility (e.g. `animate-recorrido-blink` as a Tailwind utility class won't work — only the manually written `.animate-recorrido-blink` CSS class works).
- `src/app/globals.css:102-160` — All 28 CSS custom properties used by shadcn components are defined in `:root` (light): `--background`, `--foreground`, `--card`, `--card-foreground`, `--popover`, `--popover-foreground`, `--primary`, `--primary-foreground`, `--secondary`, `--secondary-foreground`, `--muted`, `--muted-foreground`, `--accent`, `--accent-foreground`, `--destructive`, `--destructive-foreground`, `--success`, `--success-foreground`, `--warning`, `--warning-foreground`, `--border`, `--input`, `--ring`, `--chart-1` through `--chart-5`, `--sidebar` through `--sidebar-ring`, `--glass-bg`, `--glass-border`, `--glass-shadow`, `--glass-blur`, `--radius`.
- `src/app/globals.css:165-221` — All 29 variables (light set + glass vars) are properly overridden under `.dark` selector. Confirmed via grep that every var defined in `:root` is also set in `.dark`.
- `src/app/globals.css:16-97` — `@theme inline` block maps all 27 `--color-*`, `--font-*`, `--radius-*`, `--shadow-*` tokens correctly to the `var(--...)` counterparts.
- `src/components/ui/sonner.tsx:33-36` — Uses `var(--popover)`, `var(--popover-foreground)`, `var(--border)`, `var(--radius)` — all defined.
- `src/components/ui/` — All 22 components use Tailwind utility classes like `bg-background`, `text-foreground`, `border-border`, `text-muted-foreground`, `bg-primary`, `text-primary-foreground`, `bg-secondary`, `text-secondary-foreground`, `bg-destructive`, `text-destructive-foreground`, `border-input`, `ring-ring`, `bg-card`, `text-card-foreground`, `bg-popover`, `text-popover-foreground`. These all resolve through the `@theme inline` tokens.
- `src/components/ui/` — No component uses `--sidebar-*` or `--chart-*` directly via `var()`, but the `@theme inline` block defines the `--color-sidebar-*` and `--color-chart-*` tokens for potential use.
- `src/app/(driver)/driver/layout.tsx:74` — Uses `drop-shadow-[0_0_6px_var(--primary)]` — `--primary` is defined.
- `src/app/globals.css:403-409` — Print styles preserved: hides `header, aside, nav, [data-slot="sheet-trigger"], .no-print` and resets `main` padding.
- `src/app/globals.css:311-381` — Utility classes `glass`, `glass-strong`, `skeleton`, `gradient-text`, `hover-lift`, `tap-active`, `glow-ring` are all complete with dark-mode overrides for skeleton (lines 340-350) and glass variables (lines 216-220).
- `node_modules/shadcn/dist/tailwind.css` and `node_modules/tw-animate-css/dist/tw-animate.css` — Provide `accordion-down`, `accordion-up`, `enter`, `exit` keyframes plus the `animate-in`/`animate-out` utilities, and `@custom-variant` data-state selectors. No conflict with globals.css definitions.

### CHANGES
None.

### RISKS
- **Critical: `--font-sans: var(--font-sans)` circular reference** (line 65). While this likely resolves due to Tailwind v4's cascade (the `@theme inline` token `--color-font-sans` is separate from the raw `--font-sans` variable set by the Inter font), the naming collision could cause bugs on Tailwind upgrades or if the cascade order changes. Mitigation: rename the `@theme inline` token to `--font-body` or keep the raw CSS variable as `--font-sans` but use a different theme token name.
- **`--shadow-glow-sm` is defined but never used** (line 86). Unnecessary bloat in the theme. Mitigation: remove the unused token, or document where it's expected to be used.
- **No `--animate-recorrido-blink` or `--animate-glow-pulse` in `@theme inline`** (lines 89-96 end before these two). The `.animate-recorrido-blink` and `.animate-glow-pulse` CSS classes work (they're hand-written at lines 392-393), but Tailwind v4's `animate-recorrido-blink` utility class resolution won't work since there's no `--animate-recorrido-blink` token. This is inconsistent with the other 8 animations that have both the `@theme inline` token AND the `.animate-*` class. Mitigation: add `--animate-recorrido-blink: recorrido-blink 1s ease-in-out infinite;` and `--animate-glow-pulse: glow-pulse 3s ease-in-out infinite;` inside the `@theme inline` block.
- Dark mode properly overrides all 29 light-mode variables; no missing overrides detected. Print styles are present and correct.

### BLOCKERS
None.
